meaning: adj.没有缺点的,不会做坏事的
examples: David's good looks and impeccable manners had always made her blind to his faults.
She had impeccable taste in clothes.
But impeccable judgement outside off-stump and a slice or two of luck saw him through those tentative beginnings.
The IMF head has impeccable English, a great resume, and a lot of charisma.
